mechanism_narrative: Negative 3-month momentum in INDPRO signals contracting production and falling expected earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the production trend weakens.
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month INDPRO momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid downward crossing in FEDFUNDS reflects emergency policy easing driven by financial stress rather than benign discount-rate relief, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below its 3-month moving average by more than 50 bp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS cuts are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent low signals recessionary labor-market deterioration, weaker household income, and lower earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its 12-month low
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-triggered episodes do not deliver lower 60-day forward S&P 500 returns than non-triggered episodes or if the negative hit rate is below 55% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint declines in RSAFS and PAYEMS identify simultaneous weakening in consumer demand and wage income, forcing earnings downgrades and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both decline over the latest 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-PAYEMS decline does not underperform either single-series decline alone, or if 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals reduced construction activity, tighter mortgage credit, and weaker durable-goods demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by more than 15%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakdowns are not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the coefficient on HOUST downside gaps is non-negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: High FEDFUNDS combined with a rising UNRATE identifies restrictive policy colliding with labor deterioration, increasing default risk and lowering equity multiples over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above its 24-month median and UNRATE rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-UNRATE condition has no incremental negative return effect versus UNRATE alone or if 20-day excess returns are not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS is rising signals an inflation-driven tightening shock that raises discount rates and squeezes margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate rises and FEDFUNDS rises over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS joint acceleration is followed by non-negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if interaction terms are insignificant in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Positive growth in GDPC1 after a prior slowdown signals stabilization in aggregate demand and earnings expectations, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth crosses from negative to positive
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth reversals are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if out-of-sample average triggered returns are not above baseline.
